item.title: Junior Staff Post - Wikiwalk
item.content:encoded:
Timer Expired. Edits made as discussed below. Author may revert at any time.

The WikiWalk Team is proposing edits to this page. All proposed edits will be in a collapsible below this post, and after 7 days if there are no serious objections to the edits, the feedback received will be taken into account as the edits are made. If you have questions not directly related to the article about this project, please pm a member of the WikiWalk Team.

+ show block
– hide block

A couple of edits to make here.

First of all, CtM here is meant to be the author of SCP-1285. A crosslink can be added there back to 1255 (this will be more clear when my approved but pending Wikiwalk Proposal on that SCP goes through. Changing:

"CtM" is engraved upon the underside of each instance.

To:

"CtM" is engraved upon the underside of each instance.

Next, the authorial intent behind this SCP is that it is meant to be an advertisement come to life. After hearing this, I was immediately reminded of SCP-4998. A crosslink could be added. Changing this:

Attached to SCP-3428-16 was a loudspeaker. As soon as SCP-3428-16 animated, the loudspeaker began playing Night on Bald Mountain.

To this:

Attached to SCP-3428-16 was a loudspeaker. As soon as SCP-3428-16 animated, the loudspeaker began playing Night on Bald Mountain1

If anyone has any other ideas, feel free to suggest them below.

Footnotes
1. This instance of SCP-3428 had an SCP-4988 instance for █████ on it's side.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oh. Huh. Well, I got that Pepsi was probably the antagonist here, but if this is just a reference to something, I'm not familiar with it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I've edited to reflect the material used and changed "representations" to "statues" to drive home the idea that they're not hummingbird sized.

FTR, I was going for a "living pepsi commercial" vibe, but i'm not sure that's coming through

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Okay, wait.

First, what are these made out of? Like, it says they're all different, but are we talking found scrap, random wood chunks, what is the range of materials?

Second, "no larger than 5 meters"? That is way outside the normal range of a hummingbird's wingspan, and it's just kind of dropped in there. It would be very interesting to note the size of the reported instances in the log.

Lastly, I just don't really get it. There's a thread of "hummingbirds hate soft drinks", but other than that, I'm not sure what's going on, and I feel like even this comment is disjointed. :C

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I love how this all starts making sense suddenly at the end.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: A big thanks to ihp, stormbreath, and CaptainOsKirby for the help!
